title: asperula rupestris
intro: Asperula rupestris, also known as Rock Wood Sorrel or Wild Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, and is also valued for its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Asperula rupestris can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
